hook: One drink before bed might seem helpful, but it actually disrupts your sleep.

script.hook: One drink before bed might seem helpful, but it actually disrupts your sleep.
beats: Alcohol can help you fall asleep faster, but it suppresses REM sleep, which is crucial for memory and emotional processing.
Studies show that even low doses of alcohol can significantly reduce REM sleep duration and quality.
The effects can linger into the next day, impacting your overall recovery and brain health.
script.close: Follow BestKarma for science-backed health without the hype.

visualPlan: Show a glass of wine next to a bed to illustrate the common belief.
Display a brain graphic highlighting REM sleep functions.
Use a chart showing the relationship between alcohol consumption and REM sleep suppression.
End with a person checking their sleep data on a wearable device.
